chief_complaint: A 42-year-old male presented with severe abdominal pain.
history: Severe hypertriglyceridemia is a critical risk factor for acute pancreatitis. Lipemic interference in laboratory testing complicates diagnosis and delays clinical intervention.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Triglycerides: > 21.94 mmol/L, Sodium: 128 - 137 mmol/L, Total protein: 84.2 - 62.8 g/L
